common.tagline: Evidence-based genealogy at the speed of thought
common.subtagline: Bring GEDCOM, DNA matches, and archive sources into a single tree where every fact has provenance and every connection has its rationale.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.network: We couldn't reach the server. Check your connection and retry.
errors.unauthorized: You need to sign in to view this page.
errors.forbidden: You don't have permission to do that.
errors.notFound: We couldn't find what you were looking for.
errors.validation: Some of the values look off. Please review and resubmit.
errors.rateLimit: Too many requests. Slow down and retry in a moment.
errors.globalBody: An unexpected error occurred. We've logged it and will investigate.
errors.sectionBody: The rest of the app is still working — try refreshing this section, or come back later.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitleStub: Phase 4.12 placeholder. Real tree list lands in Phase 4.13.
dashboard.treeCountDescription: Phase 4.13 will populate this with the real list of trees you own or collaborate on.

notifications.settingsDescription: Choose which events you want to be notified about. Disabled events won't appear in the bell or generate any record.

evidence.title: Evidence-based genealogy
evidence.body: Every node carries provenance: which file, which import, which manual edit. No "hints" without sources, no facts you can't trace back to a record.

hypotheses.title: Hypothesis-aware
hypotheses.body: See arguments for AND against every parent-child link. Composite confidence with rule-by-rule breakdown — your judgment stays in control, automation does not auto-merge.

unified.title: DNA + GEDCOM + FamilySearch
unified.body: One picture for autosomal matches, imported pedigrees, and external archives. Cross-platform DNA matching with chromosome painting; FamilySearch OAuth pedigree pull.

easternEurope.title: Eastern Europe first-class
easternEurope.body: Russian, Ukrainian, Yiddish names handled natively. Daitch-Mokotoff phonetic search. Cyrillic ↔ Latin transliteration on import. Designed for jewish genealogy, not bolted on.

screenshots.pedigreeAlt: Screenshot of the pedigree tree visualization
screenshots.chromosomeAlt: Screenshot of the DNA chromosome painting visualization
screenshots.pedigreeCaption: Pedigree view — collapsible 10-generation tree with DNA badges and confidence indicators.
screenshots.chromosomeCaption: Chromosome painting — 22 autosomes + X with shared segments highlighted per match.

pricing.title: Simple pricing
pricing.subtitle: Free for hobby trees, Pro for serious research.

waitlist.title: Stay in the loop
waitlist.subtitle: Pricing and feature updates as they ship — no spam.
waitlist.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Try again in a moment.
waitlist.errorInvalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address.

demo.title: Live demo — sample tree
demo.subtitle: A read-only public sample tree with three generations and a synthetic DNA match. No login needed.
demo.cta: Sign up to import your own
demo.noteBody: All names and dates are synthetic. The data is shipped with the app for product demos.

onboarding.title: Welcome to AutoTreeGen
onboarding.subtitle: Three steps and you'll have a tree to explore.

step1.title: Choose a source
step1.subtitle: Where should we pull your initial pedigree from?
step1.gedcomDesc: I have a .ged file from another app (Ancestry, MyHeritage, RootsMagic, ...)
step1.familysearchDesc: Pull ancestors directly from my FamilySearch account via OAuth.
step1.blankDesc: I'll add people manually as I go.

step2.title: Import
step2.subtitleGedcom: Drop your .ged file or pick from disk.
step2.subtitleFamilysearch: We'll redirect you to FamilySearch to authorize access.
step2.subtitleBlank: Pick a name for your new tree.

step3.title: You're all set
step3.subtitle: Your tree is ready. Open it to start exploring or jump to the dashboard.

tour.restartHint: Replay the welcome tour from the beginning.
tour.restartingNotice: Tour restarted. Refresh the page to see it again.

step1.title: Welcome to AutoTreeGen
step1.body: Let's take a quick 6-step tour through the parts of the app you'll use the most. You can skip anytime.

step2.title: Import your first GEDCOM
step2.body: Bring an existing tree from Ancestry, MyHeritage, or any GEDCOM 5.5.1 / 5.5.5 file. Round-trip preserves provenance — nothing silently dropped.

step3.title: Search persons
step3.body: Find people across all your trees with phonetic (Daitch-Mokotoff) search — works for Cyrillic, Yiddish, and transliterated names alike.

step4.title: Family tree visualization
step4.body: Open any person to see a 10-generation collapsible pedigree. DNA badges, confidence indicators, and links to underlying evidence are inline.

step5.title: DNA matches
step5.body: Connect a kit (Ancestry / 23andMe / MyHeritage / FTDNA) and see your matches with shared cM and chromosome painting per match.

step6.title: Hypotheses
step6.body: Suggested same-person and parent-child links live here for review. Approve, reject, or defer — automation never auto-merges (CLAUDE.md §5).

step7.title: Share with family
step7.body: Invite collaborators to a tree (read / edit / admin) or publish a privacy-aware public link. Settings let you replay this tour anytime.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Pay only when you need power-user features. Hobby use stays free.
pricing.faq1A: Yes. DNA data is encrypted at rest, never sold, never used to train models. See our privacy policy.
pricing.faq2A: Yes — billed month-to-month, cancel from settings, your data stays accessible read-only.
pricing.faq3A: Yes — 5.5.5 is the canonical format. Older 5.5.1 and proprietary tags from Ancestry / MyHeritage / Geni are imported with round-trip preservation.

offline.banner: You're offline. Changes will sync when you reconnect.

matchDetail.linkToPerson: Link to a person in your tree

merge.title: Merge two persons (manual review)
merge.subtitle: Resolve conflicting fields side by side. Backend uses a single survivor; your per-field picks determine which side wins overall. Soft-delete is reversible for 90 days.

sides.title: Sides
sides.body: Primary on the left, candidate on the right. The survivor keeps the canonical record; the other becomes soft-deleted with a redirect.

fields.title: Resolve conflicting fields
fields.noConflicts: No conflicting fields between the two persons.
fields.namesBody: Names from the merged person move to the survivor with offset sort orders.

conflicts.none: No blocking hypothesis conflicts. Merge can proceed after manual review.
conflicts.blockedTitle: Merge blocked by {count, plural, one {1 conflict} other {# conflicts}}:

confirm.title: Confirm merge
confirm.body: This will merge {candidate}… into {primary}… (survivor: {survivor}). Soft-delete; you have 90 days to undo from the merge log.
confirm.reviewedCheckbox: I reviewed every conflicting field above and confirm this is correct.
confirm.error409: Merge blocked by hypothesis conflicts (HTTP 409). Review the conflict block above.
confirm.error422: Server rejected the request (HTTP 422). Refresh and try again.

success.title: Merge committed
success.body: Survivor {survivor}… absorbed merged person {merged}…. Undo within 90 days from the merge log.

missingCandidate.title: No candidate selected
missingCandidate.body: Open this URL with ?candidate={id} to compare two persons. Usually you arrive here from a duplicate suggestion or a hypothesis review.

log.title: Merge history
log.subtitle: All merges where this person took part. Undo is available for {days} days after the merge.
log.empty: This person hasn't taken part in any merges yet.
log.purgedAt: Hard-deleted at {time} — undo no longer possible.
log.undoErrorAlreadyUndone: This merge was already undone (server returned 409).

stats.title: Tree statistics
stats.subtitle: Aggregated counts and surname breakdown for this tree. Refreshed live — no caching.

oldest.title: Oldest known birth
oldest.description: Earliest birth-year recorded across all persons in this tree.

settings.title: Tree settings
settings.subtitle: Tree-level configuration. Self-anchor lets AI features compute relationships from your perspective.

egoAnchor.title: Yourself in this tree
egoAnchor.description: Pick the person that represents you. AI features (chat, suggestions) will then describe relationships from your perspective — "wife's brother" instead of just "brother of person X".
egoAnchor.emptyState: You haven't set yourself in this tree yet. Pick the person that represents you so AI features can compute relationships from your perspective.
egoAnchor.nonOwnerHint: Only the tree owner can set the self-anchor.

aiExtraction.panelDescription: Let Claude read this source and propose structured facts (persons, events, relationships). You review every suggestion before it reaches the tree.
aiExtraction.uploadDescription: Scan, photo, or screenshot of the source. JPEG / PNG / GIF / WEBP, ≤ 25 MB.
aiExtraction.statusNone: No AI extraction has run for this source yet.
aiExtraction.statusCompleted: Completed — {facts} new fact suggestions ready for review
aiExtraction.errorBudget: You've reached an AI extraction limit ({kind}).
aiExtraction.errorVisionRejected: Couldn't process this image. Try another file.
aiExtraction.errorGeneric: Couldn't extract facts. Please try again.

owner.title: Sharing
owner.subtitle: Invite collaborators and manage who can see this tree.
owner.forbiddenBody: Only the tree owner can manage sharing. If you think this is a mistake, ask the current owner to add you.

members.heading: Members
members.description: People with access to this tree.
members.empty: No collaborators yet — only the owner can see this tree.
members.revokeConfirm: Remove access for {email}? They will lose access immediately.

invitations.heading: Pending invitations
invitations.description: Invitations not yet accepted. Each link is valid for 14 days.

invite.heading: Invite a collaborator
invite.description: Send an email invitation. The recipient will need to sign in (or sign up) before joining.
invite.roleEditor: Editor — can add and change data
invite.messagePlaceholder: Hi! I'd love to share my family research with you.

accept.title: Accept invitation
accept.alreadyAcceptedBody: Open the tree from your dashboard.
accept.invalidBody: This link has expired or was revoked. Ask the inviter for a new one.
accept.notFoundBody: The link may be wrong or already deleted.
accept.signInPrompt: Sign in to accept this invitation
accept.signInDescription: You need an account to join the tree. Sign in or create one — it's free.
accept.emailMismatchTitle: This invitation is for a different email
accept.emailMismatchBody: Signed in as {currentEmail}, but the invitation was sent to {invitedEmail}. Sign out and sign in with the invited email to continue.

emptySupporting.title: No supporting evidence
emptySupporting.description: This link has no citations or rules yet. Adding archive searches helps anchor it.

relationshipEvidence.emptyProvenance: No import or manual-edit history recorded.
